diagrams.title: Memory Layout in C++ Process
diagrams.description: Shows virtual memory layout with stack (growth downward), heap (growth upward), static data, and code segments
elements: Free space between stack and heap

diagrams.title: new and delete Operation Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step flow of memory allocation and deallocation in C++
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the complete lifecycle of C++ memory management

diagrams.title: Class-specific vs Global Overloading Priority
diagrams.description: Shows precedence when both global and class-specific new/delete are overloaded
elements: Class operator new (highest priority for that class)
Global operator new (fallback for non-class types)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding which overload is used in different scenarios

diagrams.title: Memory Fragmentation Example
diagrams.description: Demonstrates how memory becomes fragmented over time
elements: After allocations: Block with used and free pieces
Fragmented: Many small free blocks, cannot satisfy large request
diagrams.purpose: Understanding why custom allocators like memory pools are useful

diagrams.title: Placement New Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows how placement new constructs objects at specific addresses
elements: Buffer can be reused after object destruction
diagrams.purpose: Understanding placement new for specialized memory management
